title: Compact JSON tree explorer with inline edit and path breadcrumb
design_brief: Building a JSON viewer/editor for an API debug tool (think Postman, Bruno, Insomnia, Apollo Studio). Pain point: most JSON viewers either show formatted pretty-print (long scroll) or collapse-all-by-default (clicks to drill).
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Generic chevrons that don't show node type
Path not visible during deep navigation
ux_reasoning:
Tree shows top-level expanded by default; nested objects/arrays show their key count + collapse chevron. Click value to inline-edit. Path breadcrumb at top shows current focused node (data.users[3].email). Arrow keys navigate; Enter expands/collapses; e to edit value.

Node type icons: {} for object, [] for array, prefix for strings (quotes), nothing for numbers. Color-coded muted: keys oklch(0.4 0.04 250), strings oklch(0.5 0.14 25), numbers oklch(0.45 0.16 145), null/undefined oklch(0.6 0.005 250).

design_tokens.colors: Keys oklch(0.4 0.04 250). Strings oklch(0.5 0.14 25). Numbers oklch(0.45 0.16 145). Booleans oklch(0.55 0.16 280). Null oklch(0.6 0.005 250). Focused row bg oklch(0.94 0.04 250).
design_tokens.spacing: Row: 20px tall. Indent: 16px per level. Breadcrumb: 28px tall at top.

component_architecture: <JsonTree value onEdit>. Recursive <Node /> renders based on type. Breadcrumb from focused path.
interaction_details: Top-level expanded by default. Click chevron to collapse.
Click a value: focused; path breadcrumb at top updates.
Color-coded: strings red-ish, numbers green-ish, booleans purple, null gray.
Object/array show count: {3} / [12].
accessibility_considerations: role='tree' on root, role='treeitem' on each node. Keyboard nav with arrow keys. Reduced-motion: no animations.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Top-level expanded by default — scannable without clicks. 2) Path breadcrumb updates on focus — never lost in deep nesting. 3) Color-coded by value type — semantic at a glance.

title: Tag input with comma-separates, paste-list, and backspace-removes
design_brief: Building a multi-tag input (think Github labels, Linear labels, email To: fields). Pain point: clunky add/remove UX.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Generic chips with no clear remove
ux_reasoning: Type text, comma or Enter commits as a tag chip. Comma in pasted text auto-splits. Backspace at empty input removes the last chip. Click chip X to remove. Cmd+A selects all chips.

design_tokens.colors: Chip bg: oklch(0.95 0.005 250). Chip text: oklch(0.3 0.005 250). Chip X: oklch(0.55 0.005 250).
design_tokens.motion: Chip add: 80ms scale-in. Chip remove: 80ms fade.

component_architecture: <TagInput value onChange separator>.
interaction_details: Type + comma/Enter: commits tag.
Paste comma-separated text: auto-splits into chips.
Backspace at empty input: removes last chip.
Click chip X to remove single tag.
accessibility_considerations: Input has aria-label. Each chip X has descriptive aria-label. Reduced-motion: chip-in animation disabled.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Backspace-at-empty removes last chip — keyboard-only flow. 2) Paste-with-commas auto-splits — supports bulk paste. 3) Comma OR Enter commits — either separator works.

title: Live form with field-level lock indicators and conflict prevention
design_brief: Building shared forms (think Google Forms responses live-editing, Airtable). Pain point: two users editing same field at the same time = lost data.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Hard-lock entire form when one user is editing
Silent overwrite of other user's changes
ux_reasoning: When a user focuses a field, a soft lock is broadcast — other users see that field with a colored ring + name chip. Others CAN still type into it but the value commits to last-writer. The original user gets a brief 'Bob is also editing this' indicator. On their next blur, conflict is presented inline with diff view + Keep mine / Use theirs.

design_tokens.colors: Focus ring user color: 2px. Conflict diff: oklch(0.96 0.04 60) bg.
design_tokens.motion: Ring color shift: 150ms. Conflict diff slide-in: 150ms.

component_architecture: <CollabFormField value onChange onFocus />. Uses presence context for other users' focus state.
interaction_details: Focus field: broadcast lock to other users.
Others see field in your color + 'You editing' chip.
If conflict detected on blur: inline diff with Keep mine / Use theirs.
accessibility_considerations: Field has proper label. Conflict diff uses role='alert'. Reduced-motion: transitions become instant.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Soft lock with visible focus ring — not a hard lock, but discouraging. 2) Inline conflict resolution (Keep mine / Use theirs) — never a modal. 3) 'X editing' chip appears only when another user is in the field.

title: Pull-to-refresh on web with elastic over-scroll and threshold release
design_brief: Building mobile-web pull-to-refresh (think iOS Mail, Twitter). Pain point: most web implementations lack the elastic resistance that makes the gesture feel native.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Refreshing animation that holds too long
Triggering at scroll position other than top
ux_reasoning: At scroll-top, downward pull translates content with rubber-band resistance (1.0 ratio first 60px, then 0.4). Visual indicator (small circle) grows in 'arming' until threshold (80px), then fills as confirmed. Release past threshold: refresh fires. Release before: snap back.

design_tokens.colors: Indicator: oklch(0.55 0.18 250) outline, fills on threshold.

component_architecture: <PullToRefresh onRefresh children>.
interaction_details: At scroll top: pull-down translates content with resistance (1.0 first 60px, 0.4 after).
Indicator grows; fills at 80px threshold.
Release past threshold: refresh fires (spinner during async).
Release before threshold: snap back.
accessibility_considerations: Indicator aria-hidden. Refresh state announced via aria-live='polite'. Reduced-motion: snap animation disabled.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Two-phase resistance (1.0 then 0.4) — feels native, not 1:1. 2) Indicator fills at threshold — visual confirmation before release. 3) Pure CSS / pointer events, no library.

title: Sankey diagram for user flow with hover-to-trace path
design_brief: Building a Sankey for product analytics (think Mixpanel funnel flow, Heap user paths). Pain point: Sankeys are dense and hard to follow without interaction.
ux_reasoning: Hover a node or flow: that path through the diagram highlights (others dim to 20%). User can trace 'where did people who hit X go next'. Limit to 4-5 stages so it stays readable.

design_tokens.colors: Default flow opacity: 0.6. Highlighted: 1. Dimmed: 0.2.
design_tokens.typography: Node label: 12px medium. Count: 11px tabular oklch(0.55).

component_architecture: <Sankey stages flows>. Uses d3-sankey for layout calc.
interaction_details: Hover a node: that node + flows in/out highlight; rest dim to 20%.
Hover a flow: that flow + its endpoints highlight.
Flow thickness encodes value.
accessibility_considerations: Underlying data in sr-only table. Each flow/node has aria-label. Reduced-motion: transitions instant.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Hover-to-trace lets users follow specific user paths through the funnel. 2) Dimming non-path keeps focus on the trace. 3) Flow thickness encodes magnitude — visual at a glance.

title: Permissions request UX with rationale-first prompting
design_brief: Building permission requests (camera, microphone, notifications) for a web app. Pain point: most apps trigger native permission dialog without context, getting denied.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Triggering native dialog on page load
No way to recover from denial
ux_reasoning: Before calling navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia or Notification.requestPermission, show a small inline RATIONALE first ('Allow camera to record video messages'). User clicks Continue to trigger the native dialog. If denied: graceful inline explanation with link to browser settings.

design_tokens.colors: Rationale bg: oklch(0.97 0.005 250) with 1px border. Denied state: oklch(0.95 0.04 25) bg.

component_architecture: <PermissionRequest kind onGranted>. Tracks state across mounts (idle, prompting, granted, denied).
interaction_details: Rationale shown FIRST before native dialog.
Click Continue: triggers native permission flow.
Denied: inline guidance to browser settings.
accessibility_considerations: Rationale is real text, properly labeled. Continue is a real button. Denied state uses role='alert'.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Rationale before native dialog — drastically improves grant rate. 2) Denied state has actionable recovery path. 3) Inline (not modal) — feels like part of the feature.

title: Password input with strength meter and rule checklist
design_brief: Building password input for signup (think Apple ID, Stripe). Pain point: generic strength bars don't tell users WHAT to fix.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Validation that fires on submit only
Hide password by default with no toggle
Generic 'must contain a number' below field
ux_reasoning: Show password rules as a checklist below the input. Each rule (8+ chars, 1 uppercase, 1 number, 1 symbol) has a check icon that fills as the rule is met. Strength meter is a 3-segment bar (weak/medium/strong). Show/hide toggle inside input on right.

design_tokens.colors: Rule met: oklch(0.55 0.14 145). Unmet: oklch(0.55 0.005 250). Strength weak: oklch(0.6 0.18 25). Medium: oklch(0.7 0.16 80). Strong: oklch(0.55 0.14 145).
design_tokens.motion: Rule check: 150ms ease-out. Strength bar: 200ms width.

component_architecture: <PasswordInput rules value onChange>. Each rule { label, test: (v: string) => boolean }.
interaction_details: Each rule checks live as user types.
Strength bar fills + colors based on count.
Show/hide toggle inside input.
accessibility_considerations: aria-describedby links rules + strength. role='status' on changes. Reduced-motion: transitions instant.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Checklist tells user WHAT to fix — not just 'weak'. 2) Rule checks fill as met — positive feedback. 3) Show/hide toggle inside the input — never separate field.

title: Interactive 3D model viewer with auto-rotate and gesture controls
design_brief: Building a 3D model viewer for an e-commerce product detail page (think Sketchfab embedded, Apple's AR Quick Look on web). Pain point: 3D viewers are often heavy and break on touch devices.
ux_reasoning:
Show static product image instantly. When 3D model loads, crossfade in. Auto-rotate at slow speed; pause on touch/click. Drag rotates; pinch zooms. Double-tap toggles auto-rotate.

Mobile: prominent 'View in AR' button if supported.

design_tokens.colors: Bg: oklch(0.97 0.005 250) (neutral product backdrop). UI controls: oklch(0.18 0.005 250) at 60% on hover.
design_tokens.spacing: Canvas: full container. AR button: 48px tall bottom-center mobile.

component_architecture: <ModelViewer src previewImageUrl arUrl>. Uses model-viewer (web component) or @react-three/fiber.
interaction_details: Static preview shows instantly. 3D crossfades in on load (400ms).
Auto-rotate at slow speed. Touch pauses; release resumes after 2s.
Pinch zooms; drag rotates.
View in AR button on mobile (when supported).
accessibility_considerations: alt attribute on viewer. AR button has aria-label. Reduced-motion: auto-rotate disabled.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Preview image shows BEFORE 3D loads — no loading state. 2) Uses model-viewer web component — well-tested cross-device. 3) AR button only on supported devices — graceful degradation.

title: Filter UI: bottom-sheet wheel-picker on mobile, dropdown on desktop
design_brief: Building filter controls (think Airbnb dates, Apple Sports). Pain point: same dropdown on mobile is hard to tap.
ux_reasoning: Desktop: filter pills opening dropdown popovers. Mobile: same pills, but tapping opens a bottom-sheet picker (iOS wheel or large checkbox list). Reset button always visible.

design_tokens.colors: Pill bg: oklch(0.95 0.005 250). Active: oklch(0.94 0.04 250). Sheet bg: white.

component_architecture: <FilterPill label options selected onChange />. Modality-detected popover vs sheet.
interaction_details: Desktop: pill opens dropdown popover with checkboxes.
Mobile: pill opens 50vh bottom sheet with large 17px checkbox rows.
Both: reset button at bottom.
Pill label updates with count when selected.
accessibility_considerations: Real buttons, real checkboxes. Sheet on mobile is role='dialog' with focus trap.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Same filter primitive, two paradigms — popover on desktop, sheet on mobile. 2) 17px option text on mobile — easily tappable. 3) Reset always visible.

title: Tabs primitive following ARIA 1.2 with manual activation
design_brief: Building a tabs primitive (think Radix UI, Reach UI). Pain point: many implementations use automatic activation (focus = select) which is unfriendly for SR users.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No focus visible on tab triggers
Tab panel not announced as related
ux_reasoning: Follow WAI-ARIA APG tabs with MANUAL activation: arrow keys move focus only; Enter or Space activates. Home jumps to first; End to last. Tab moves to active panel content.

design_tokens.colors: Active tab: bottom border 2px oklch(0.55 0.18 250). Inactive: oklch(0.55 0.005 250).

component_architecture: <Tabs activeId onChange><Tab.List><Tab id label /></Tab.List><Tab.Panel id /></Tabs>.
interaction_details: Tab to enter tablist. Arrow keys move FOCUS only (not selection).
Enter or Space activates the focused tab.
Home/End jump to first/last tab.
Tab again moves into the active panel.
accessibility_considerations: Full WAI-ARIA 1.2 conformance. Tested with all major screen readers. Reduced-motion: no animations to disable.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) MANUAL activation (focus ≠ selection) — keyboard users explore tabs without triggering panels. 2) Home/End for first/last — full spec coverage. 3) Single tab stop into the tablist (roving tabindex).

title: Image with content-aware aspect-ratio placeholder and dominant-color fallback
design_brief: Building an Image primitive (think Next.js Image, Unsplash). Pain point: layout shift on image load + blank gray during load.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Single gray color regardless of image
ux_reasoning: Aspect-ratio container set from image metadata. Background = dominant color of image (computed at build/upload time). Image fades in over 100ms when loaded.

design_tokens.colors: Bg: image's dominant color (single hex per image).

component_architecture: <Image src width height dominantColor alt sizes srcset>.
interaction_details: Aspect-ratio set BEFORE image loads — no shift.
Dominant color fills slot until image loads.
Image fades in 100ms on load.
Real srcset + sizes for responsive.
accessibility_considerations: Real alt attribute. Reduced-motion: 100ms fade is already minimal.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Dominant color as bg — instant 'something is there' that hints at content. 2) Aspect-ratio prevents layout shift. 3) Fast (100ms) fade — perceptually instant.

title: Newspaper editorial layout with multi-column text and pull quotes
design_brief: Building a news article layout (think Bloomberg's longform, NYT special projects, Quartz cards). Pain point: most 'newspaper-style' web layouts are just centered single column.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Pull quotes that float right with generic style
Drop cap that's the default browser one
Sub-heads in same style as body
ux_reasoning: Real newspaper feel: 3-col layout on wide screens (collapsing gracefully), authored multi-line drop cap, italicized pull quotes pulled into their own column with em-dash attribution, dateline + byline at top in caps.

design_tokens.colors: Ink: oklch(0.15 0.005 30). Paper: oklch(0.985 0.003 80) (warm paper).
design_tokens.typography: Body: serif (Tiempos Text or Source Serif) 16px / 24px. Headline: serif display 36px. Byline: sans 11px uppercase. Drop cap: 56px serif.
design_tokens.spacing: 3 columns gutter 32px on wide. Pull quote: indent right with 1px rule.

component_architecture: <NewspaperArticle headline byline dateline body>. CSS columns for body. <PullQuote /> styled inline.
interaction_details: 3-col body on wide; 1-col on mobile via CSS columns.
Drop cap floats from first paragraph.
Pull quote pulled inline with em-dash attribution.
Dateline + byline in caps tracking-widest.
accessibility_considerations: Real semantic HTML. Pure typography, no animations. Reading order matches DOM.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) CSS columns (not flex tricks) — real multi-column flow. 2) Drop cap with custom serif display face. 3) Pull quote with top+bottom rule + em-dash attribution — editorial detail.

title: Compact code review file tree with diff stats sidebar
design_brief: Building a file tree for a PR review view (think Github Files tab, Graphite, Reviewable). Pain point: file trees often show paths but no diff context.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Plain file tree with no diff stats
Tree that doesn't show review status
ux_reasoning: Each file row shows: collapsible folder chevron, filename (truncated), +/- line counts as colored badges, status dot (reviewed/unreviewed/conflict). Click file: opens diff. Right side has tiny sparkline of diff size.

design_tokens.colors: Add badge: oklch(0.55 0.14 145). Remove badge: oklch(0.6 0.18 25). Reviewed dot: oklch(0.55 0.14 145). Unreviewed: oklch(0.7 0.005 250).

component_architecture: <FileTreeWithStats files activeFile onFileSelect>. Each file: { path, additions, deletions, status }.
interaction_details: Each row: status dot + filename + +N -M diff counts.
Click file: opens diff.
Status dot: green (reviewed), red (conflict), gray (unreviewed).
accessibility_considerations: Buttons with proper labels. Status has aria-label. Reduced-motion: no animations.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) +/- counts visible per file without click. 2) Review status dot at left — scannable. 3) Monospace for path alignment.

title: Searchable settings with deep-link to specific control
design_brief: Building settings search (think macOS System Preferences search, Slack settings search). Pain point: settings are dozens of pages; users can't find specific controls.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Search only matches setting label, not description
ux_reasoning: Cmd+/ inside settings opens search. Indexes label + description + tags. Results show category + setting. Click result: navigates to that page AND highlights the specific control with a brief glow.

design_tokens.colors: Highlight glow: 2px ring oklch(0.55 0.18 250) for 1500ms.

component_architecture: <SettingsSearch index onNavigate>. Each index entry { categoryId, settingId, label, description, tags }.
interaction_details: Cmd+/ opens search. Type to filter.
Results show setting + category context.
Enter or click: navigates to that setting + highlights it for 1500ms.
Esc closes search.
accessibility_considerations: Full keyboard nav. role='listbox'/'option'. Reduced-motion: highlight glow is brief (1500ms) — could simplify to instant in reduced mode.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Tag-indexed (not just label) — find 'dark mode' even if setting is called 'Appearance'. 2) Navigates + highlights specific control — closes the discovery loop. 3) Cmd+/ shortcut — power-user discoverable.

title: Inline comment threads in a doc with reply collapse and resolution
design_brief: Building inline comments (think Google Docs, Notion, Linear's issue comments). Pain point: many comment systems lose context when collapsed.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Comments in sidebar only (lose spatial anchor)
ux_reasoning: Comments anchor to text selections in the doc. Thread shows as a side margin annotation; click expands. Replies threaded. 'Resolve' collapses thread to a tiny dot but keeps it accessible.

design_tokens.colors: Comment anchor highlight: oklch(0.95 0.08 60) bg in doc. Resolved dot: oklch(0.65 0.005 250).

component_architecture: <InlineCommentThread anchor comments onReply onResolve>.
interaction_details: Selection in doc gets highlight bg; thread bubble in right margin.
Click bubble to expand/collapse.
Resolve collapses to a small dot — still clickable to view history.
Reply textarea inline at thread bottom.
accessibility_considerations: Thread is article. Comments are real. Reply textarea labeled. Reduced-motion: open animation disabled.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Resolve collapses to a dot — preserved without clutter. 2) Replies threaded inline. 3) Anchor highlight in doc stays even when thread collapsed.

title: Confirmation dialog with delete-name-typing for irreversible actions
design_brief: Building destructive action confirmations (think GitHub repo delete, Stripe account delete, Linear workspace delete). Pain point: 'Are you sure?' modals are too easy to dismiss.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Modal where button is auto-focused for accidental Enter
ux_reasoning: For TRULY destructive (delete project, etc): require typing the object's name verbatim into a confirmation input. Delete button stays disabled until match. This 'cognitive lock' (Stripe's pattern) prevents accidental destruction.

design_tokens.colors: Destructive: oklch(0.55 0.18 25). Input match indicator: oklch(0.55 0.14 145) check.
design_tokens.typography: Modal title: 17px medium. Name to type: mono 13px.

component_architecture: <DestructiveConfirm objectName onConfirm onCancel>.
interaction_details: Modal opens with input autofocused. Cancel is focused by default for accidental Enter.
Type exact name to enable Delete button.
Check icon appears when match.
Esc cancels.
accessibility_considerations: Modal role + aria-modal + label. Input has descriptive aria-label. Disabled Delete button has aria-disabled.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Type-to-confirm — cognitive lock against accidental clicks. 2) Check icon on match — positive feedback. 3) Default focus on input (not Delete) — prevents Enter-to-delete misfires.

title: Network graph with force layout, focus-neighborhood and search
design_brief: Building network/graph visualization (think Obsidian's graph view, Bloomberg's connections graph). Pain point: force layouts wobble forever and become hairballs.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No way to focus on a node
ux_reasoning: Run force simulation for ~200 ticks at mount, then freeze. Click a node: camera centers + highlights 1-hop neighbors; others dim 20%. Search input fuzzy-matches node labels.

design_tokens.colors: Nodes: oklch(0.55 0.18 250) (or category-colored). Focused: brighter. Dimmed: 0.2 opacity. Edges: oklch(0.7 0.005 250) 1px.

component_architecture: <NetworkGraph nodes edges>. Uses d3-force precomputed positions.
interaction_details: Force layout run once, frozen.
Click node: that node + 1-hop neighbors highlight; others dim.
Search input fuzzy-finds nodes and centers.
accessibility_considerations: Underlying sr-only adjacency list. Each node aria-labelled. Reduced-motion: opacity instant.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Layout computed once + frozen — no infinite wobble. 2) Focus mode hides clutter. 3) Search jumps to node.

title: Empty list with one mock item as 'tap to begin'
design_brief: Building empty state for a task/issue list. Pain point: most empty states have an illustration + CTA button.
ux_reasoning: Empty list shows ONE mock row styled exactly like a real one, with placeholder italic text ('Tap to add your first task'). Tapping it focuses an inline input that creates the real task on Enter. The mock IS the affordance.

design_tokens.colors: Mock row italic text: oklch(0.55 0.005 250).

component_architecture: <EmptyList renderRowInput onCreate>.
interaction_details: Tap the mock row: becomes an inline input.
Enter: creates real task. Esc: cancels.
No modal, no separate CTA.
accessibility_considerations: Real button + input. Reduced-motion: no change.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Empty state IS the affordance — italic placeholder text on a mock row. 2) Inline edit on tap. 3) No illustration, no CTA button.

title: Credit card input with brand detection and formatted display
design_brief: Building credit card input (think Stripe Elements, Square). Pain point: bare 16-digit input feels insecure.
ux_reasoning: Single input formats digits as user types ('1234 5678 9012 3456'). Brand icon (Visa, Mastercard, Amex) appears inline as soon as detectable. CVC + expiry as separate small inputs to the right. Luhn validation on blur.

design_tokens.colors: Brand icon area: 32px wide left of input.

component_architecture: <CardInput onChange>. Detects brand from first digits.
interaction_details: Type digits: live format with spaces, brand-aware.
Brand icon appears inline left of number.
Expiry + CVC inline right.
Auto-tab between fields based on completion length.
accessibility_considerations: Each input has aria-label + autoComplete attribute. inputMode='numeric' for mobile.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Live brand detection from first digits. 2) Single visual unit with brand + number + expiry + CVC — feels like one card. 3) Amex format detection (15 digits, 4-digit CVC).

title: Card stack with depth on hover for product showcase
design_brief: Building a card stack animation for a 'Made with X' grid (think framer.com showcase, vercel.com customers). Pain point: most card grids are flat and feel like Bootstrap.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Flat cards with shadow on hover
ux_reasoning: Each card has subtle perspective tilt that responds to cursor position over it. Hovering deepens the parallax (background image moves faster than foreground content). Releases naturally.

design_tokens.colors: Card bg: white. Subtle shadow on tilt.
design_tokens.motion: Tilt: 1:1 with cursor over card. Release: 400ms ease-out spring.

component_architecture: <TiltCard><Background /><Foreground /></TiltCard>. CSS transform-style: preserve-3d.
interaction_details: Hover card: subtle 3D tilt follows cursor.
Foreground content (title) sits closer; background image sits further → parallax effect.
Release: smooth return to flat (400ms).
No hover scale, no shadow change.
accessibility_considerations: Real card is a link/button. Reduced-motion: tilt disabled.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) CSS perspective + preserve-3d — real 3D, not pseudo-shadow. 2) Background and foreground at different Z — true parallax. 3) Smooth ease-out return — no bounce.

title: Reader-mode view that strips chrome on mobile and adds side notes on desktop
design_brief: Building an article reader (think Safari Reader, Medium reader mode, Instapaper). Pain point: mobile readers are great; desktop readers waste screen space.
ux_reasoning: Mobile: standard reader-mode — body text in serif, max-width 640px, generous line-height. Desktop: same body column, but left + right rails for marginalia (notes, definitions, related links). Toggle reader mode with Esc to exit.

design_tokens.colors: Bg: oklch(0.99 0.005 60). Text: oklch(0.18 0.005 60). Marginalia: oklch(0.45 0.005 250).
design_tokens.spacing: Body column: 640px max. Marginalia: 200px each side (desktop only).

component_architecture: <ReaderMode article notes>. Layout adapts to viewport.
interaction_details: Mobile: single-column reading.
Desktop: 3-col grid (left margin, body, right notes).
Esc exits reader mode (back to article view).
Generous serif typography on both.
accessibility_considerations: Real article + h1 + body. Marginalia in <aside>. Reduced-motion: enter fade disabled.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Desktop USES the extra space for marginalia — not just wider. 2) Mobile and desktop share body column width — typography rhythm preserved. 3) CSS grid handles layout, no JS.

title: Disclosure (details/summary) with smooth height animation and keyboard
design_brief: Building a Disclosure / Collapsible primitive (think Radix Collapsible). Pain point: HTML <details> doesn't animate; custom impls often forget keyboard.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: JS toggle that hides display: none (no animation)
Animation that breaks during rapid clicks
ux_reasoning: Use real <button> + ARIA. Content height animates from 0 to scrollHeight via the new interpolate-size: allow-keywords + transition (modern CSS), with fallback to JS height measurement.

design_tokens.colors: Trigger hover: oklch(0.97 0.005 250).

component_architecture: <Disclosure trigger><Content /></Disclosure>.
interaction_details: Click trigger: smooth 250ms height animation.
Triangle rotates 90deg.
aria-expanded reflects state.
Keyboard: Enter/Space activates.
accessibility_considerations: Real button + aria-expanded + aria-hidden on collapsed content. Reduced-motion: no animations.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Smooth height animation via measurement (no display:none). 2) Triangle rotation = visual + ARIA confirm state. 3) Works with rapid clicks (height stays measured).

title: Optimistic data refresh with stale-content tag
design_brief: Building a data list view that refreshes (think Twitter timeline pull-to-refresh, Vercel deployments). Pain point: users see same data and don't know if it's fresh.
ux_reasoning: List shows last-updated time at top ('Updated 2m ago'). Background polling re-fetches; if new data arrives, shows 'X new items above' pill that user can tap to merge in. List doesn't jump.

design_tokens.colors: Pill bg: oklch(0.18 0.005 250). Stale tag: oklch(0.55 0.005 250).

component_architecture: <RefreshableList items lastUpdatedAt onRefresh>.
interaction_details: Sticky 'Updated Ns ago' shows freshness.
If new items arrive: 'X new · tap to show' pill appears.
Tap pill: merges new items in.
List never jumps.
accessibility_considerations: Pill is a real button with aria-label. Update time has role='status'.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Stale time visible — user knows freshness. 2) Pill-to-merge — explicit consent, no scroll jump. 3) Background polling + manual merge separates concerns.

title: Cyberpunk neon-grid loading screen with scanlines and glitch
design_brief: Building a stylized loading screen for a video game launcher or sci-fi app (think Cyberpunk 2077 UI, Apple TV+ trailers, Severance opening titles).
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Generic spinner with cyberpunk color palette
ux_reasoning: Subtle CRT scanline overlay (low alpha). Magenta + cyan accents on dark navy background. 'NOW LOADING' in a wide condensed sans (futura/orbitron) with brief glitch flicker every 4-6s. Progress as a gradient bar.

design_tokens.colors: Bg: oklch(0.10 0.04 280). Cyan: oklch(0.7 0.18 200). Magenta: oklch(0.65 0.22 320). Scanline: oklch(0 0 0 / 0.08).
design_tokens.typography: Display: 'Orbitron', 'Rajdhani', condensed sans 32px uppercase tracking-widest. Mono: JetBrains Mono 11px.
design_tokens.motion: Scanline: static. Glitch: 200ms keyframes triggered every 4-6s random.

component_architecture: <CyberLoader progress label>.
interaction_details: Title in cyan with magenta/cyan chromatic split during glitch frames.
Progress bar gradient cyan→magenta.
CRT scanline overlay at low alpha (decorative).
Glitch every 4-6s random.
accessibility_considerations: Reduced-motion: glitch and any animation disabled. Progress as real number for SR. Color: percentage text is supplementary.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Real chromatic aberration glitch on title — text-shadow trick. 2) CRT scanline at 8% alpha — peripherally felt, never distracting. 3) Cyan→magenta gradient bar — period-correct cyberpunk palette.

title: Build log viewer with collapsible sections and timing per step
design_brief: Building a CI/CD log viewer (think GitHub Actions, Vercel deployments, CircleCI). Pain point: logs are giant streams; finding the failed step takes scrolling.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Flat log with no section structure
Time stamps in raw ISO format
No way to jump to failed step
Section headers indistinguishable from log lines
ux_reasoning: Build phases as collapsible groups. Each phase shows: status icon (success/failed/skipped), name, duration, expand chevron. Failed phases auto-expand. Click phase: expand/collapse. Logs within phase are mono. Click 'Jump to failure' jumps to first failed phase.

design_tokens.colors: Success: oklch(0.55 0.14 145). Failed: oklch(0.6 0.18 25). Skipped: oklch(0.55 0.005 250). Mono bg: oklch(0.16 0.005 250) text light.
design_tokens.spacing: Phase row: 32px tall. Log line: 18px tall.

component_architecture: <BuildLog phases />. Each phase { name, status, duration, lines[] }.
interaction_details: Phases collapsed by default; failed phases auto-expanded.
'Jump to failure' sticky banner at top.
Click phase header: expand/collapse with height animation.
Status icon + duration visible per phase.
accessibility_considerations: Real buttons. Status icons have aria-label. Reduced-motion: expand animation disabled.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Failed phases auto-expand — surface the problem. 2) Jump-to-failure sticky banner — one-click to the issue. 3) Per-phase duration — see what's slow.

title: List with j/k vim navigation and visible focused-row indicator
design_brief: Building list keyboard nav (think Linear issues, Gmail inbox). Pain point: many lists support arrow keys but not vim aliases that power users want.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Focused-row indicator only visible with focus visible mode
No way to open with Enter
ux_reasoning: List has single tabindex (the container). j/k or arrow keys move focused row. Focused row has a 2px left border. Enter opens; e or x for shortcuts. Tab into list once; not per-row.

design_tokens.colors: Focused row: 2px left border oklch(0.55 0.18 250). Bg subtle oklch(0.97 0.005 250).

component_architecture: <KeyboardList items renderItem onOpen>.
interaction_details: j/k or arrow keys move focused row.
Enter or o opens.
Custom shortcuts via prop (e for archive, x for select, etc.).
Single tab stop into container.
accessibility_considerations: role='listbox'/'option', aria-selected. Single tabindex on container is canonical for vim-style lists.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) j/k AND arrows — vim users and traditional users both happy. 2) Custom shortcuts via prop — extensible. 3) Visible focused-row indicator survives mouse + keyboard.

title: Status update broadcast (away/busy/active) with auto-detection
design_brief: Building user status indicators (think Slack status, Linear's online, Linear's away). Pain point: manual status updates get stale.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Disrupting other users with status notifications
ux_reasoning: Auto-detect via: page-visibility (hidden = away after 5min), input idle (no keyboard/mouse for 10min = away), explicit manual override (sets and locks for 4h). Other users see colored dot on avatar + optional status text.

design_tokens.colors: Active: oklch(0.55 0.14 145). Away: oklch(0.75 0.16 80). Busy: oklch(0.6 0.18 25). Offline: oklch(0.7 0.005 250).
design_tokens.spacing: Status dot: 8px circle on avatar bottom-right.

component_architecture: useUserStatus() hook auto-detects + manages manual override.
interaction_details: Active = recent input + tab visible.
Auto-away after 10min idle or 5min tab hidden.
Manual override locks for 4 hours.
Dot color encodes status.
accessibility_considerations: Dot has aria-label. Status changes broadcast via aria-live='polite'. Reduced-motion: color transition becomes instant.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Auto-detection via 3 signals (input idle, visibility, manual). 2) Manual override expires after 4h — never stays stale. 3) Single hook abstracts all signals.

title: Copy-to-clipboard button with brief tick + revert
design_brief: Building a copy button for code blocks, share links, etc. (think GitHub copy code, Stripe API examples). Pain point: most copy buttons give no feedback OR show a tooltip that lingers.
ux_reasoning: Button starts as copy icon. On click: icon morphs to check (200ms), label changes to 'Copied'. After 1500ms: reverts to copy icon, label gone. Single small interaction.

design_tokens.colors: Idle: oklch(0.55 0.005 250). Confirmed: oklch(0.55 0.14 145).

component_architecture: <CopyButton text>.
interaction_details: Click: icon morphs to checkmark over 200ms, color shifts to green.
After 1500ms: reverts to copy icon.
aria-label updates to 'Copied' during confirmed state.
No tooltip, no toast.
accessibility_considerations: aria-label reflects current state. Reduced-motion: color transition becomes instant.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Inline state — no toast pollution. 2) Auto-revert after 1500ms — never sticky. 3) Icon swap is the entire feedback.

title: Sparkline grid for at-a-glance trend comparison
design_brief: Building a sparkline grid for a metrics overview (think Stripe overview, Datadog grid view). Pain point: comparing 20 trends at once is hard.
ux_reasoning: Grid of small sparklines (120x40 each). Each shows: label, current value, sparkline with endpoint dot, % change vs previous period. All share visual conventions (no axes, no legends). Click any: opens detailed chart.

design_tokens.colors: Sparkline stroke: oklch(0.55 0.005 250). Endpoint dot: brand. Delta positive: green. Negative: red.
design_tokens.motion: Hover lift: 1.02 scale. NO chart animations.

component_architecture: <SparklineGrid metrics />. Each metric { label, value, format, trend[], delta }.
interaction_details: Grid of small sparklines, each with label + current + delta + trend.
Click tile: opens detailed chart.
Direction-aware delta color (revenue-up green, churn-down green).
accessibility_considerations: Each tile is a button with aria-label including value + delta. SR alternative provides full data.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) At-a-glance trend grid — scan 12 metrics in 3 seconds. 2) Endpoint dot anchors the current value. 3) Direction-aware delta colors.

title: Inline 'what's new' callout for newly-added features
design_brief: Building feature-launch notifications (think Linear's 'NEW' badges, Notion's What's New). Pain point: changelog modals interrupt work; users miss new features.
ux_reasoning: Newly-added features get a small NEW badge next to their menu item OR button label. Hover/focus reveals a small popover with what's new + 'Learn more' link. Badge auto-dismisses after first click on the feature OR after 14 days.

design_tokens.colors: NEW badge: oklch(0.55 0.18 250) bg, white text.
design_tokens.motion: Badge pulse on first appearance only (single 1s pulse).

component_architecture: <NewFeatureBadge featureId launchedAt children>. Uses localStorage to remember dismissal.
interaction_details: NEW badge inline next to feature label/icon.
Auto-dismisses on first interaction with the feature.
Auto-dismisses after 14 days regardless.
Hover for description.
accessibility_considerations: Badge has title attribute. Activation logic is on interaction with the wrapped feature. No modal.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Auto-dismiss on first use — never sticky. 2) 14-day cap — never lingers. 3) Inline (not modal) — no interruption.
